headline: Airline Shift To GEnx Engines Puts GE Valuation In Sharper Focus
summary: British Airways and Air New Zealand have selected General Electric's GEnx engines for their Boeing 787 Dreamliner fleets. These airlines are shifting away from competing Rolls-Royce engines, citing reliability concerns. The moves reflect growing airline interest in GEnx across the widebody aircraft engine market. For investors watching General Electric (NYSE:GE), this shift in airline engine choices adds context to a stock that has already seen a very large 3 year return and is up 30.7%...
